LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT N.A.D., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 13, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 22, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT N.A.D., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ASSUMING THAT THE BROAD LANGUAGE OF THE RELEASE APPLIES TO N.A.D., INC., PLAINTIFF ESTABLISHES MUTUAL MISTAKE PURSUANT TO THE DECLARATION OF MR. DO. THAT DECLARATION IS UNCONTROVERTED AND CLEARLY SHOWS THAT THE ONLY CLAIMS TO BE RELEASED RELATED TO HERTZ CORPORATION. N.A.D. INC. WAS NOT EXPRESSLY NAMED IN THE RELEASE, N.A.D., INC. AND ITS INSURER WERE NOT PART OF THE NEGOTIATION PROCESS REGARDING THE RELEASE, AND THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE CONTRACTING PARTIES INTENDED TO INCLUDE IT AS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY. SEE HESS V. FORD MOTOR CO., (2002) 27 CAL.4TH 516, 527-531. THE FACTS OF LAMA V. COMCAST CABLEVISION (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 59, ARE DISTINGUISHABLE. THERE, THE PARTY RELYING ON THE RELEASE PRESENTED EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE CONTRACTING PARTIES INTENDED TO RELEASE ALL POTENTIAL TORTFEASORS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-13-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN C. DO, TEL. NO. (408) 287-4444 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DANIEL MCCRARY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BEVERLY E. NARAYAN, TEL. NO. 227-2323 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT N.A.D., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Assuming that the broad language of the release applies to N.A.D., Inc., plaintiff establishes mutual mistake pursuant to the declaration of Mr. Do. That declaration is uncontroverted and clearly shows that the only claims to be released related to Hertz Corporation. N.A.D. Inc. was not expressly named in the release, N.A.D., Inc. and its insurer were not part of the negotiation process regarding the release, and there is no evidence that the contracting parties intended to include it as a third party beneficiary. See Hess v. Ford Motor Co., (2002) 27 Cal.4th 516, 527-531. The facts of Lama v. Comcast Cablevision (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 59, are distinguishable. There, the party relying on the release presented evidence showing that the contracting parties intended to release all potential tortfeasors. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2014 Continued To Nov-13-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT N.A.D., INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to November 13, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 22, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
